description: probe an OpenAI-compatible remote LLM/VLM endpoint (BASE_URL/v1/models) and verify the selected model id is advertised before pointing a deploy at it
rules: read-only: only GETs BASE_URL/v1/models; never mutates the endpoint or any deploy artifact
if REMOTE_API_KEY is set it is sent as a Bearer token (use $NVIDIA_API_KEY for build.nvidia.com)
accepts both the OpenAI list shape ({data:[{id}]}) and a single-model object ({id})
when the endpoint advertises multiple models and no EXPECTED_MODEL is given, it errors and lists them so the user can choose one
verifies EXPECTED_MODEL is actually advertised, not just that the endpoint is reachable
usage: Set BASE_URL (e.g. https://integrate.api.nvidia.com or http://localhost:30081) and optionally EXPECTED_MODEL; set REMOTE_API_KEY for authenticated endpoints. Output: models.json (audit) + models.json.log.
limitation: Network-dependent: offline or for an unreachable endpoint it reports degraded. Probes advertised models only; it does not test inference, latency, or context length. Endpoint/model selection is owned by the chosen profile.
